title: Traffic
description:
Write an AI to identify which traffic sign appears in a photograph, using TensorFlow to build a convolutional neural network trained on the German Traffic Sign Recognition Benchmark (GTSRB) dataset of 43 sign categories.

In `traffic.py`, complete `load_data` and `get_model`:

- `load_data(data_dir)` reads every image (using OpenCV `cv2`), resizes each to `IMG_WIDTH` x `IMG_HEIGHT`, and returns `(images, labels)` where labels are the integer category numbers. Use `os.path.join` / `os.sep` for platform independence.
- `get_model()` returns a compiled `tf.keras` model whose input shape is `(IMG_WIDTH, IMG_HEIGHT, 3)` and whose output layer has `NUM_CATEGORIES` units. You choose the convolutional/pooling/hidden/dropout layers.

(Uses cv2, scikit-learn, and tensorflow.)

**Example:**
```
$ python traffic.py gtsrb
Epoch 1/10
500/500 [===...] - loss: 3.71 - accuracy: 0.15
...
333/333 - 5s - loss: 0.16 - accuracy: 0.95
```
